text:
There is a specific kind of freeze.
It is the freeze of knowing everything and still not being able to move.
I remember the bedside. Crackles on auscultation. The pathway was right there. Cardiomyopathy. Pulmonary edema. The fluid, the failing pump, the reason the lungs were filling. I had read it. I had memorized every step.

But I had never walked it on a real person before.
There is something that happens in that gap. Between what you know and what your hands are willing to do. The knowledge sits in your head. The patient is in front of you. And there is this terrible distance between the two.

If I had seen that case ten times before in a simulation, it would have been easy. The hands would have known. But I had not. So I froze.
Because knowing the words was not the same as knowing the person in front of me.

Nobody tells you that gap exists until you are standing in it.
I did not have a name for it yet.
